title: local/declare/export with a command substitution swallows its failure under set -e
rule: Under set -e, `v=$(cmd)` aborts when cmd fails, but `local v=$(cmd)`, `declare v=$(cmd)`, `export v=$(cmd)`, and `readonly v=$(cmd)` do NOT — the line's exit status is the builtin's (almost always 0), which swallows the substitution's failure (also breaking any following `$?` check). This is ShellCheck SC2155. Fix: declare and assign on separate lines (`local v; v=$(cmd)`) so errexit and `$?` see cmd's real status.
triggers: set -e not exiting on failed command substitution
function continues after failed command in local assignment
$? is 0 after local assignment that should have failed
